titleStmt.title: A letter Mr Mooney to his sister Katie Mooney, 20 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Mr Mooney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Mr Mooney is sending a letter to Katie asking about how things are at home and catching up on the latest news.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
5th (b) Reserve Brigade R.F.A
Ballincollig. Dec 19 20 1915 Dear Katie, A few things I want
you to send me when you
go to Dublin are the following
tooth powder and the usual
tobbaco & two pair of
Pumerals & of course the
socks & Putties. I will
be glad to have some of
the Christmas pudding.
It will be something from
home.
Did Tommy have a ride on
the motor bike since I
hope it is in good order
still. I am sure the kitchen
is grand now also the
lobby. I will not know
the place when I get home.
We are having a hard
frost now. I suppose poor
old Barry will run away
from me when he next sees
me. I nearly forgot to ask for
little Lot. I have no
more to say at present so
Good—Bye & God Bless you
all.
5th (b) Reserve Brigade R.F.A
Ballincollig. Dec 19 20 1915 Dear Katie, A few things I want
you to send me when you
go to Dublin are the following
tooth powder and the usual
tobbaco & two pair of
Pumerals & of course the
socks & Putties. I will
be glad to have some of
the Christmas pudding.
It will be something from
home.
5th (b) Reserve Brigade R.F.A
Ballincollig. Dec 19 20 1915 Dear Katie, A few things I want
you to send me when you
go to Dublin are the following
tooth powder and the usual
tobbaco & two pair of
Pumerals & of course the
socks & Putties. I will
be glad to have some of
the Christmas pudding.
It will be something from
home.
